summary: This is a brief introductory post linking to the third part of Joel Spolsky's 'Great Design' series. It teases the question of what separates best-in-class products from their competitors, using examples like the iPod, Aeron chair, and Brad Pitt. The post itself is essentially a pointer to the longer essay rather than a standalone argument.
key_points: Every product category has standout leaders that define excellence
The post sets up an inquiry into what common qualities make certain products great
This is part of a larger series exploring principles of great product design
money_quotes: Just about every product category has its blue-chip, gold-plated stars.
key_insight: Understanding what separates the best products from the rest requires examining the common qualities shared by category-defining leaders.
